You can use DFS, BFS, or Union-Find to solve this problem.
For DFS/BFS: Build an adjacency list. Iterate through all nodes 0 to n-1. If a node hasn't been visited, increment your component count and run DFS/BFS to visit all connected nodes.
For Union-Find: Initialize n separate sets. For each edge, union the two sets. The number of components is n minus the number of successful unions.
